read: TileLang now has concrete reduced-write dKV branches in the experiment probe. The correct branch is prepacked_scalar_reduced, which owns compact active rows and writes non-atomic partial rows, but it is group-owned scalar math and is far too slow at S4096. The intended route_mfma_reduced branch keeps query-major MFMA contribution math and static compact-row blocks, but the current implementation compiles and runs with NaN output, so it is not correct yet. The actual-LI Triton sink floor shows that removing selected-row writes alone is insufficient: the current producer body plus route traversal is still about 8.38 ms at S4096.

tilelang_prepacked_scalar_reduced.contract: prepack compact active rows to (token, selected) pair lists; one CTA owns compact output rows; no dynamic output slot and no global atomic in the partial producer
tilelang_prepacked_scalar_reduced.decision: Correct but not a speed path. Keep only as a TileLang lowering/correctness baseline for static row-owned reduced writes.

tilelang_route_mfma_reduced.contract: one CTA owns a static compact-row block; compute selected-row dKV contributions with TileLang GEMM; reduce matching route slots into the compact row block; write compact partial rows without atomics
tilelang_route_mfma_reduced.failure: candidate output checksum and diff are NaN even after using the vendored FullCol GEMM policy and fragment-to-shared staging
tilelang_route_mfma_reduced.decision: Compiled but not correct. Next work should isolate the MFMA contribution body with the exact vendored sparse-MQA input layout, then reintroduce static route-block reduction.

actual_li_triton_sink_floor.contract: keep current query-major dot-K producer and route-slot traversal, but replace selected-row partial/dKV writes with one diagnostic sink vector per micro tile
actual_li_triton_sink_floor.decision: The next TileLang speed candidate must improve the contribution schedule as well as writeback. A reduced-write wrapper around the current producer is unlikely to beat the 8 ms wall.

next_kernel_ladder: Add a TileLang MFMA contribution-body sink that mirrors the vendored sparse-MQA layout exactly and proves finite output.
Once finite, add static route-block reduction with row_block=16 and fragment/shared staging.
Only after correctness, scale to S4096 and compare against the 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic wall.
